zh.lifeSummary: Eme Agnes Richmond於1477年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾。她於1498年與John Cox the obscure IV在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1567年在英國英格蘭維爾爾華阿登享年90歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素可聖 瑪麗 馬格達倫。

nameMeanings.name: Richmond
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from Richmond (North Yorkshire). The placename was probably transferred after the Norman Conquest from any of numerous places in France named with Old French riche ‘mighty, strong’ + munt, mont ‘mountain, hill’. Richmond in southwest London received this name only in the reign of Henry VII, in honor of the king, who had been Earl of Richmond until he came to the throne, and is unlikely to be the source of this surname.
occasionally a variant of Richman , with excrescent -d. The two names were probably often confused.

variants.name: Richman
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Richeman, either from an unrecorded Old English Rīcmann, or from a pet form of Richard + the hypocoristic suffix -man. The name was sometimes interchangeable with Rickman , especially in recent times.
English: occasionally perhaps a nickname from Middle English riche ‘rich, powerful’ + -man. Compare Rich .
English: perhaps a variant of   Richmond  , or vice versa.

nameMeanings.name: Eme
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Esmé . The change of -s- to -d- may have been due to the influence of the coexisting names Esmond and Edmund . In spite of its accent, it is not found as a French name.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: Eme Agnes Richmond was born in 1477, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. She married John Cox the obscure IV in 1498, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1567, in Whaddon, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 90, and was buried in St Mary Magdalen, Sussex, England, United Kingdom.

ko.St Mary Magdalen, Sussex, England, Unite: 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 스트 메리 막그다렌
ko.lifeSummary: Eme Agnes Richmond(은)는 1477년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1498년 영국에서 John Cox the obscure IV(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1567년 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 화아던에서 90세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 스트 메리 막그다렌에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Eme Agnes Richmond родилась в 1477 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Cox the obscure IV в 1498 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1567 г. в местности уаддон, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 90 лет и была похоронена в местности ст мэри магдеилен, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания.
